crashReporter.dialog.title: Send crash report?

@crashReporter.dialog.title.description: Title of the dialog shown on the next launch after Cmdr crashed, asking permission to send a crash report.

crashReporter.dialog.body: Cmdr quit unexpectedly last time. Here''s a crash report with details that can help fix this.

@crashReporter.dialog.body.description: Main explanation in the crash-report dialog. Reassuring and matter-of-fact. "Cmdr" is the product name (do not translate).

crashReporter.dialog.privacyNote: It includes the app version, macOS version, and which part of the code crashed. No file names or personal data.

@crashReporter.dialog.privacyNote.description: Privacy reassurance in the crash-report dialog, listing what the report contains and confirming it has no personal data. "macOS" is Apple's OS name (do not translate).

@crashReporter.dialog.reportIdLabel.description: Label before the short crash-report identifier. The identifier itself follows in a code-styled badge and is not part of this string.

crashReporter.dialog.reportIdHelp: Mention this if you reach out about the issue.

@crashReporter.dialog.reportIdHelp.description: Small helper line under the crash-report ID, telling the user to quote the ID if they contact support.

@crashReporter.dialog.showDetails.description: Toggle that expands a panel showing the raw crash-report JSON. Preceded by a disclosure triangle.

@crashReporter.dialog.copy.description: Button that copies the raw crash-report JSON to the clipboard. Swaps to the "copied" confirmation for two seconds after a click. Keep it short (a tight button).

@crashReporter.dialog.copied.description: Confirmation shown for two seconds on the copy button right after the crash report is copied to the clipboard. Keep it short (a tight button).

@crashReporter.dialog.alwaysSend.description: Label for a checkbox that opts the user into sending future crash reports automatically without asking.

crashReporter.dialog.attachEmail: Attach my email ({email}) so we can reply

@crashReporter.dialog.attachEmail.description: Label for an opt-in checkbox to include the user's email so the team can reply. Shown only when the user has an email on file; their actual address is shown in the parentheses.

placeholders.email: the user's email address on file (e.g. alex@example.com)

@crashReporter.dialog.dismiss.description: Button that closes the crash-report dialog without sending. Keep it short (a tight button).

@crashReporter.dialog.send.description: Primary button that sends the crash report. Keep it short (a tight button).

@crashReporter.dialog.sending.description: Label on the send button while the crash report is being sent (button is disabled meanwhile). Ends with three dots. Keep it short.

crashReporter.sentToast.message: Crash report sent. Thanks for helping improve Cmdr.

@crashReporter.sentToast.message.description: Toast confirming the crash report was sent, thanking the user. "Cmdr" is the product name (do not translate).

@crashReporter.sentToast.changeSettings.description: Button on the sent-confirmation toast that opens the app's Settings to the Updates section. "Settings" and "Updates" are names of in-app navigation locations; keep them consistent with how those sections are named elsewhere in the app.
